varieties.name: Arbequina
varieties.traits: Small, compact tree (4-5m); dense bushy growth; small dark green leaves; small round fruit (2-3g)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Small fruit with minimal pit; fruity, mild oil with ripe apple, banana notes; smooth, creamy texture
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity; self-fertile; early fruiting (4 years); mature trees yield 15-20kg
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent adaptation to Brisbane's subtropical climate; tolerates humidity and warm winters without chill requirement; widely proven in SE Queensland; suitable for containers; low maintenance
varieties.availability: Widely available at Brisbane nurseries, Pine Mountain Nursery, Brookfield Gardens, quality plant suppliers

varieties.name: Manzanillo
varieties.traits: Low spreading tree (5m); compact growth habit; medium-sized plump fruit (3-5g); dual-purpose
varieties.flesh_fruit: High flesh-to-pit ratio; juicy, sweet flesh; excellent for table olives and oil; rich green oil with slight tartness
varieties.yield_notes: Regular annual bearer; high productivity; early ripening

varieties.name: Frantoio
varieties.traits: Medium tree (6-8m); vigorous growth; medium fruit (2-3.5g); self-fertile
varieties.flesh_fruit: High-quality fruity, aromatic oil; nutty flavor when pickled; robust, complex flavor profile
varieties.yield_notes: Consistently heavy yields; reliable producer; high oil content

varieties.name: Kalamata
varieties.origin: Kalamata region, Greece (PDO protected, ancient Greek variety)
varieties.traits: Large dark purple to black fruit; almond-shaped; distinctive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Large, meaty flesh; rich, fruity flavor; ideal for table use; high oil content
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity; prefers long hot summers
varieties.growing_notes: Can thrive in Brisbane with good drainage and air circulation; tolerates warm winters; needs well-draining soil; popular for home cultivation; typically cured in brine or red wine vinegar

varieties.name: Helena (St Helena)
varieties.origin: St Helena Island, Moreton Bay, Brisbane (planted 1880s from Europe, believed to be Frantoio type; 140+ years Queensland heritage)
varieties.traits: Tall slender crown; dark bottle-green leaves (darker than other varieties); fast-growing; self-pollinating; medium fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Small to medium green oval fruits rich in oil; won Australia's first international agricultural award; exceptional quality oil historically exported to Italy
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable fruiting in SE Queensland; proven productivity over 140 years

varieties.name: Picual
varieties.origin: Jaén, Spain (most widely grown olive for oil production, traditional Spanish variety)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit (3-4g); early bearing; vigorous growth; largely self-fertile
varieties.flesh_fruit: Buttery, smooth texture; high natural antioxidants; herbaceous aroma with tropical fruit notes; low acidity
varieties.yield_notes: High yields; high oil content; mid to late season ripening
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited to Brisbane warm subtropical climate; cold tolerant; excellent heat resistance making it perfect for butter replacement in recipes; versatile for everyday cooking; resistant to hot conditions

varieties.name: Koroneiki
varieties.origin: Crete, Greece (3,000+ years cultivation, 'Queen of Olives')
varieties.traits: Small fruit (2-2.5g); smaller than Arbequina; medium vigor; spreading habit; thick small leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Rich green oil; aromatic with wheatgrass, sweet almond hints; herbaceous, grassy flavor; high polyphenols; exceptional health benefits
varieties.yield_notes: High oil content; good yields; suitable for high-density planting
varieties.growing_notes: Performs in warm climates; requires extra care but produces premium quality oil; flavor stability over time; excellent for salads, yogurt, fish

varieties.name: Leccino
varieties.origin: Tuscany, Italy (principal Italian oil variety, 200+ years)
varieties.traits: Small to medium brown-purple fruit; early producer; good pollinator
varieties.flesh_fruit: Rich, buttery, delicate oil; sweet spicy taste; meaty flesh; milder than Frantoio
varieties.yield_notes: High oil content; reliable production; common Italian oil variety
varieties.growing_notes: Versatile and adaptable; performs well in various climates; often blended with Frantoio, Pendolino; suitable for Brisbane subtropical conditions

varieties.name: Coratina
varieties.origin: Puglia (Apulia), Italy ('Gold of Apulia', accounts for 40% Italian oil production)
varieties.traits: Medium vigor; dense spreading growth; large elongated fruit; very adaptable
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pungent, complex, herbaceous oil; high polyphenols; exceptional stability; green pepper, grass aroma
varieties.yield_notes: High yield; good quality; easily extracted oil
varieties.growing_notes: Flourishes in warm climates; tolerates cold and moderate drought; strong oil for blending; excellent for meat dishes, bruschetta

varieties.name: Barnea
varieties.traits: Medium fruit; bears young; self-fertile; dual-purpose
varieties.flesh_fruit: Medium to high oil content; can be pickled; good table and oil variety
varieties.yield_notes: High yields; mid-season ripening
varieties.growing_notes: Performs well in warm subtropical climates; recommended for Brisbane area; suitable for warmer regions

varieties.name: Pendolino
varieties.origin: Tuscany, Italy (traditional Italian variety, renowned pollinator)
varieties.traits: Weeping growth habit; dense abundant crown; produces large amounts of fertile pollen
varieties.flesh_fruit: Delicate, pleasant oil with subtle fruitiness; almond, apple, grass notes; mild characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: High fruit production; moderate oil yield; primarily used as pollinator (10% of orchard)

varieties.name: Mission
varieties.origin: California Missions, 1700s (possibly Picholine Marocaine from Morocco; 250+ years American heritage)
varieties.traits: Strong vigor; erect growth; small fruit; dual-purpose
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild, grassy flavor; good for pickling green or black; quality oil production
varieties.yield_notes: Good production; reliable bearer
varieties.growing_notes: Prefers warmer areas; tolerates cold except damp coastal conditions; historic California variety; adaptable to various climates

varieties.name: Picholine
varieties.origin: Gard, Provence, southern France (most common French cultivar, 200+ years)
varieties.traits: Medium size; spreading habit; medium density; dual-purpose; partially self-fertile
varieties.flesh_fruit: Easy pulp-to-pit detachment; fruity oil with hint of bitterness; elegant vegetal, grass notes; famous cocktail olive
varieties.yield_notes: Constant high producer with adequate irrigation; medium oil content
varieties.growing_notes: Hardy and adapted to variety of conditions; lye-cured then brine-fermented for table use; most important French variety; used in Morocco for oil

varieties.name: Ascolana (Ascolano)
varieties.origin: Marche and Tuscany, Italy (traditional Italian variety)
varieties.traits: Very large light green fruit; vigorous tree; erect growth; high cold tolerance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Large, fleshy drupes; minimal bitterness; excellent for pickling; tropical fruit, peach notes when overripe
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy bearer; medium yields; late ripening
varieties.growing_notes: Cold-hardy; requires pollinator (Leccino, Pendolino); resistant to peacock spot and olive knot; excellent table variety

varieties.name: Hojiblanca
varieties.origin: Spain (third most popular Spanish variety, 'white leaf')
varieties.traits: Silvery white leaf undersides; hardy tree; generous size fruit; dual-purpose
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm bite; slightly sweet with pleasant bitterness; almond flavor; green and floral oil
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; lower oil content than Picual/Arbequina but excellent for table use
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent frying oil; great for bread, pasta, pastries; self-fruitful but benefits from pollinators; adaptable and visually appealing

varieties.name: Sevillano (Queen of Spain)
varieties.traits: Large table fruit (9-11g); very cold resistant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Meaty texture; excellent for pickling; large size perfect for stuffing
varieties.yield_notes: Medium yields; early to mid-season ripening
varieties.growing_notes: Usually pickled green before first frost; requires pollinators (Manzanillo, Arbequina); ideal for large table olives

varieties.name: UC13A6 (Californian Queen)
varieties.origin: University of California breeding program, USA (1960s)
varieties.traits: Very large almost round table fruit; excellent flesh-to-pit ratio
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent texture and flavor when processed; primarily for table use
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields for table production
varieties.growing_notes: Developed specifically for table olive production; large attractive fruit; best pickled green

varieties.name: Barouni
varieties.origin: Tunisia, North Africa (traditional North African variety)
varieties.traits: Large table fruit (~7g); spreading tree; dual-purpose
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent texture and flavor; good for table and oil
varieties.yield_notes: Medium yields; mid to late season
varieties.growing_notes: Suits warm to cold climates; cold tolerant; suitable for hand picking; recommended for warm climates including Brisbane area

varieties.name: Correggiola
varieties.traits: Medium fruit; bears young; vigorous growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: High quality oil; fruity character
varieties.yield_notes: High yields; high oil content
varieties.growing_notes: Performs well in warm climates; good for oil production; suggested for Stanthorpe cooler region but adaptable

varieties.name: Del Morocco
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit; hardy and flexible; opposite climate to Brisbane (wet winters, dry summers native)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Distinct bitter-fruity taste with spicy kick; aromatic oil
varieties.yield_notes: Good production; can be grown in pots
varieties.growing_notes: Incredibly hardy despite opposite climate pattern; adaptable to Brisbane conditions; suitable for home gardens and containers

varieties.name: Arecuzzo
varieties.traits: Medium fruit; reliable performer
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality oil
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable yields
varieties.growing_notes: One of most reliable varieties for warmer climates; specifically recommended for SE Queensland; performs well in subtropical conditions

varieties.name: South Australian Verdale
varieties.origin: France - Australian selection (adapted to Australian conditions)
varieties.traits: Large table fruit (5-8g); medium to large oval; cold resistant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flavor and excellent texture; large seed but very good pickled quality; dual-purpose
varieties.yield_notes: Medium yields; inclined to alternate cropping but heavy 'on' years
varieties.growing_notes: Easy to harvest; usually pickled green; produces largest fruit of Verdale types; Australian-selected variety

varieties.name: Bambalina (Ornamental)
varieties.traits: Compact, naturally rounded form; striking appearance; dwarf variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Minimal fruit production; primarily ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Very low fruiting
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for hedging or feature planting in courtyard gardens; ideal for small spaces and decorative purposes; thrives in Brisbane subtropical conditions

varieties.name: Mini Olea (Dwarf Ornamental)
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety; perfect for pots and small spaces; dense silvery foliage
varieties.flesh_fruit: Doesn't produce fruit as prolifically as larger varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Low fruit production
varieties.growing_notes: Stunning ornamental feature; ideal for balconies, patios, small gardens in Brisbane; primarily grown for appearance

varieties.name: Indigenous Alternative: Ceylon Olive
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Sri Lanka (native to Western Ghats and southern India)
varieties.traits: Medium to large evergreen tree (15-60m height); large spreading crown; white flowers; smooth ovoid green fruits (2.5cm long)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sour taste; excellent for pickling; popular street food in Sri Lanka; both ripe and unripe fruit harvested August-October
varieties.yield_notes: Good fruiting in monsoon climate

varieties.name: Barnea
varieties.traits: Medium fruit; early bearing; drought resistant; heat tolerant

varieties.name: Arbequina
varieties.growing_notes: Imported to Rajasthan 2007; relatively adaptable to warm conditions; still requires some winter chill; NOT suitable for Kerala's year-round heat and monsoon

varieties.name: Koroneiki
varieties.growing_notes: Imported to Rajasthan 2007; high-quality oil producer; requires Mediterranean climate; NOT suitable for tropical Kerala

varieties.name: Pendolino
varieties.growing_notes: Maintained by Indian state agencies; excellent pollinator; requires temperate climate; NOT for Kerala monsoon region

varieties.name: Messinese
varieties.growing_notes: Maintained by state agencies; Sicilian heritage variety; requires Mediterranean conditions; experimental only

varieties.name: Moralio (Moraiolo)
varieties.growing_notes: Maintained by J&K Centre; fruity flavorful variety; wind resistant; requires cooler temperate climate

varieties.name: Cipressino
varieties.growing_notes: Maintained by J&K Centre; requires temperate conditions; experimental variety
